name: Encinar de Humienta — circular desde Burgos por el Camino de la Lana
region_intro: Mountain biking around Burgos offers a diverse landscape characterized by rolling hills, river valleys, and extensive holm oak forests. The region features a mix of historical paths, including sections of the Camino de Santiago, and dedicated trails that traverse agricultural lands and natural areas. Mountain bikers can expect varied terrain, from riverside routes to paths exploring archaeological sites. Best mountain bike trails around Burgos The most popular mountain bike trail is Encinar de Humienta — circular desde Burgos por el Camino de la Lana, a 22.5 miles (36.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route explores holm oak forests along the Camino de la Lana. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Atapuerca Archaeological Site Route 02, a moderate 25.2 miles (40.6 km) path. This route leads through the historic Atapuerca region, known for its archaeological significance. Local mountain bikers also love the French Way and Arlanzón River Loop, a 18.2 miles (29.3 km) trail leading through river valleys and sections of the French Way, often completed in about 2 hours 13 minutes. Mountain biking around Burgos is defined by holm oak forests, river valleys, and historical paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate routes to more challenging trails. The routes in Burgos are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 390 reviews. More than 3000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Burgos's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there in Burgos?
A: There are over 220 mountain bike routes available in the Burgos area, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Burgos?
A: Yes, Burgos offers approximately 35 easy mountain bike routes, which are generally suitable for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride.

Q: What about more challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders seeking a challenge, Burgos features around 23 difficult mountain bike trails that provide more technical terrain and demanding climbs.

Q: What is the typical distance and duration of mountain bike trails around Burgos?
A: The routes vary significantly, but many popular moderate trails, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100176453/encinar-de-humienta-circular-desde-burgos-por-el-camino-de-la-lana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinar de Humienta — circular desde Burgos por el Camino de la Lana&lt;/a&gt;, are around 22.5 miles (36 km) and can take about 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. Other routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103244024/atapuerca-archaeological-site-route-02" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site Route 02&lt;/a&gt; are longer, at 25.2 miles (40.6 km).

Q: Are there family-friendly mountain bike trails in Burgos?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' routes aren't explicitly tagged, the 35 easy mountain bike routes in the region are generally suitable for families and those looking for less strenuous rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Burgos?
A: Mountain biking around Burgos offers a diverse landscape. You can expect rolling hills, scenic river valleys, and extensive holm oak forests. Trails often traverse historical paths, including sections of the Camino de Santiago, and agricultural lands.

Q: Are there circular mountain bike routes available in Burgos?
A: Yes, many routes in Burgos are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103287545/french-way-and-arlanzon-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way and Arlanzón River Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a scenic circular ride.

Q: What can I see along the mountain bike trails in Burgos?
A: Many trails pass by significant points of interest. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103244024/atapuerca-archaeological-site-route-02" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site Route 02&lt;/a&gt; leads through the historic Atapuerca region, known for its archaeological significance, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; itself. You might also encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6041261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lone Tree on the Horizon&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3849773" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carcedo Lagoons&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy the most about mountain biking in Burgos?
A: The mountain bike routes in Burgos are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 390 reviews. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the mix of historical paths, and the beautiful natural landscapes, particularly the holm oak forests and river valleys.

Q: Are there any routes that follow historical paths?
A: Yes, several mountain bike routes in Burgos incorporate historical paths. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1100176453/encinar-de-humienta-circular-desde-burgos-por-el-camino-de-la-lana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Encinar de Humienta — circular desde Burgos por el Camino de la Lana&lt;/a&gt; explores holm oak forests along the Camino de la Lana, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1103287545/french-way-and-arlanzon-river-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way and Arlanzón River Loop&lt;/a&gt; includes sections of the French Way.

Q: Can I find routes that explore the Way of El Cid?
A: Yes, you can explore sections of the Way of El Cid by mountain bike. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1109986191/gr-160-way-of-el-cid-burgos-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;GR 160 - Way of El Cid: Burgos Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 22.9-mile (36.8 km) route that allows you to experience this historic path.
tips: Entertaining trail through the Humienta holm oak. In general there are no very technical sections, just some rocks. You have to be careful with some of the branches of the oaks that are quite low.
Tunnel of approximately 500 meters on the road to Santiago de la Lana. It is advisable to carry a front light on the bicycle, since you can hardly see the road.
Nice stretch of track along the Vía Verde of the Camino de Santiago de la Lana as it passes through the outskirts of Burgos. A fairly flat path suitable for all levels.

waypoints.name: Tunnel on the Vía Verde Santander–Burgos
